mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income measured by RSAFS increases consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 2.0 percent, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and growth in industrial production indicate a tightening labor market expanding manufacturing capacity, which historically precedes equity rallies and yields positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in walk-forward testing, or SPX 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference when both proxies move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above 108.0 indicates robust supply-side capacity expansion that supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains values above 108.0 for consecutive months, or the signal's predictive rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll growth exceeding 150,000 jobs sustains aggregate demand and validates earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns across all market conditions.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations where PAYEMS growth exceeds the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratios fall below zero over multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts decline exceeding 100,000 units signals weakening construction demand and broader economic softening, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops below 1200.0, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge in a walk-forward backtest spanning multiple years.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real disposable income and falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real purchasing power without inflationary pressure, which historically compresses equity risk premiums and yields positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive alpha when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day windows, or forward returns show no significant difference across signal deciles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contraction below 17200.0 billion chained dollars confirms an active recession that erodes corporate earnings and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed GDP contractions, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% in out-of-sample testing.
